_comment: MeshflowBackend = 段階委譲 (verification-routed escalation), the AI-native organizational form from soshiki-genron (experiments/meshflow.py, PAPER §6.5), as a gama backend. Try the cheap LOCAL tier first; accept its answer ONLY if the external `verify` passes; otherwise escalate to a stronger tier; mesh the drafts at the edge; gate high-stakes-unresolved tasks to a human (<<NEEDS_HUMAN>>). Fill ssh_host. Works for BOTH `gama run "<task>" --config examples/meshflow.example.json --task-type code_implementation` AND `gama bench --backends meshflow,ssh-openai --config examples/meshflow.example.json --tier large` (bench threads each case's checker as the gate, so escalation is measured honestly). The win to look for: meshflow matches the single strong tier at LOWER cost — structure, not scale.
